data.title: Priceline releases 2026 travel report: focus on exploration, connection, spontaneity. 7 travel behaviors revealed.
data.description: Travelers in 2026 are leaning towards increased exploration, connection, and spontaneity in their journeys. Priceline recently unveiled its 2026 Where to Next? Travel Trends Report on Oct. 16, 2025, highlighting seven key travel behaviors. The report indicates a surge in travelers seeking more immersive experiences, with an emphasis on embracing new destinations and forming deeper connections with local cultures. This shift is reflected in the rising number of travelers looking for authentic, off-the-beaten-path experiences. The report reveals a notable increase in last-minute bookings, suggesting a growing trend towards spontaneous travel decisions. With these evolving travel preferences, the industry is witnessing a shift towards more adventurous and authentic travel experiences.

data.title: Agriculture Drones Market to hit $10.76B by 2030, up from $2.63B in 2025 at 32.6% CAGR - MarketsandMarkets™ via PRNewswire.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of October 16, 2025, the Agriculture Drones Market is valued at an estimated 2.63 billion and is expected to soar to USD 10.76 billion by 2030, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 32.6% projected from 2025 to 2030. The increasing demand for agricultural drones is a significant driver in this sector's growth.

data.title: Kraken acquires small exchange for $100M, preparing for US derivatives trading launch with regulatory approval. No set timeline disclosed.
data.description: Kraken has acquired the license to provide regulated derivatives trading in the United States. The specific timeline for the launch of this service was not disclosed.

data.title: Dollar General offers affordable holiday meal solutions for the season in Goodlettsville, Tenn.
data.description: Embrace the holiday season with budget-friendly meal options from Dollar General in Goodlettsville, Tennessee. Enjoy affordable solutions for your festive gatherings.

data.title: Garmin introduces new OnBoard system with wireless Man Overboard detection and engine cutoff for enhanced boating safety.
data.description: A new system for detecting man overboard and cutting off engines wirelessly has been introduced, providing boaters with additional reassurance. Garmin, a leading marine electronics manufacturer, revealed this integration with their chartplotters in Olathe, Kansas on October 16, 2025. As the world's largest and most innovative company in this sector, with a presence on the NYSE under the ticker symbol GRMN, it aims to enhance safety on the water for all users.

data.title: United Airlines reports revenue of $15.225 billion, up 2.6% Y/Y. Adjusted EPS beats estimates at $2.78. Loyalty revenue up 9%.
data.description: United Airlines Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: UAL) saw a decline in premarket trading on Thursday following the release of their mixed third-quarter results. The company reported a 2.6% year-over-year increase in revenue to $15.225 billion, falling short of the consensus estimate of $15.325 billion. However, their adjusted EPS for the quarter came in at $2.78, surpassing analyst expectations of $2.62 per share. Premium cabin revenue rose by 6% Y/Y, basic economy revenue increased by 4% Y/Y, loyalty revenue was up by 9% Y/Y, and cargo revenue saw a 3% Y/Y growth. Capacity also expanded by 7.2% Y/Y. Total revenue per available seat mile (TRASM) decreased by 4.3%, while cost per available seat mile (CASM) dropped by 2.8% Y/Y. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BD commemorates 65 years of manufacturing excellence in Broken Bow, NE. Headquarters of BD for the past 65 years.
data.description: In Broken Bow, Nebraska, on October 16, 2025, BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) (NYSE: BDX), a prominent global medical technology firm, celebrates a significant milestone of 65 years of top-notch manufacturing operations at its facility. This location in Broken Bow serves as a vital component of BD's widespread U.S. manufacturing operations.

data.title: Cloud telephony market to reach $42.60B by 2030, growing at 9.8% CAGR, driven by AI integration & remote work trends.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of October 16, 2025, it has been reported that the market for cloud telephony services is expected to increase from USD 26.69 billion in 2025 to USD 42.60 billion by 2030, showcasing a steady growth rate with a CAGR of 9.8%. This rise in market value is primarily attributed to the transition of enterprises from conventional PBX systems to cloud-native solutions, reflecting the evolving trends in communication technology.

data.title: Report: Trump's family gains $1B in crypto funds during second term, backed by memecoins TRUMP and MELANIA.
data.description: According to reports, ventures in cryptocurrencies associated with the Trump family have yielded profits exceeding $1 billion. World Liberty Financial and memecoins supported by the family, such as TRUMP and MELANIA, have been highlighted as the driving forces behind this significant financial success.

data.title: Bitcoin stabilizes above $110,000 as Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin dip. $94M outflows from BTC ETFs, $5.3M inflows for ETH ETFs. 171,971 traders liquidated in past 24 hours, $417.87M losses. Quarter-over-quarter, public companies holding Bitcoin rose by 40%. Van de Poppe advises buying the dip, uptrend remains intact.
data.description: Despite $94 million in net outflows from spot BTC ETFs on Wednesday, Bitcoin is maintaining stability around $110,000. On the other hand, Ethereum ETFs saw $5.3 million in net inflows. A Bitwise report revealed a 40% increase in the number of public companies holding Bitcoin in Q3. In the past 24 hours, 171,971 traders were liquidated, resulting in total losses of $417.87 million. Analyst Michael van de Poppe highlights that Bitcoin's monthly chart indicates a healthy trend with no signs of a peak or bear market, encouraging investors to buy the dip. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Lennox acquires NSI Industries' HVAC division to enhance portfolio with DuroDyne and Supco brands, benefiting customers.
data.description: Lennox, a prominent provider of energy-efficient climate control solutions, has broadened its range of HVAC parts and supplies by incorporating the DuroDyne and Supco brands into its portfolio. This strategic move aims to enhance the company's ability to cater to the needs of both residential and commercial customers. The announcement was made in Dallas on October 16, 2025. Lennox, listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol LII, finalized the expansion to better serve its clientele.

data.title: CMC to acquire Foley Products Company, becoming third largest in the US in precast industry with strong EBITDA margins.
data.description: The recent acquisition has significantly expanded CMC's precast platform, establishing CMC as the third-largest company in the United States. This acquisition has also positioned CMC as a top player in the Mid-Atlantic and Southeast regions. The addition of this top-tier precast operator has enhanced CMC's portfolio, bringing with it industry-leading EBITDA margins and a strong cash flow profile.

data.title: DeriW experiencing rapid growth after mainnet launch, redefining perp DEX standards, becoming a CEX rival with increasing spotlight.
data.description: In Hong Kong on October 16, 2025, DeriW, a decentralized futures trading platform developed by CoinW, has experienced significant expansion since its mainnet introduction. The perp DEX sector is gaining attention, with rapid development driven by a growing influx of users.

data.title: Deel raises $300M in funding led by Ribbit Capital, valuing the company at $17.3B, following record performance.
data.description: In New York, Deel, a prominent global HR and payroll platform, revealed a significant development today. The company secured a noteworthy $300 million strategic investment from new backer Ribbit Capital, alongside existing partners Andreessen Horowitz and Coatue Management, and further backing from General Catalyst, Green Bay Ventures, and others. Following this investment, Deel is now valued at $17.3 billion. This funding round comes after a stellar year for Deel, with September 2025 marking the third consecutive year of outstanding performance for the company.

data.title: Tapestry, Inc. will discuss fiscal 2026 Q1 results via conference call at 8:00 a.m. (ET) on Nov. 6, 2025. Call ID: 7269452. Website: www.tapestry.com/investors.
data.description: Tapestry, Inc. will be conducting a conference call on November 6, 2025, at 8:00 a.m. (ET) to discuss its fiscal 2026 first quarter results. These results will be released earlier that morning via a press release. To participate in the corporate call, dial 1-866-847-4217 or 1-203-518-9845 and enter Conference ID 7269452. An audio webcast can be accessed at www.tapestry.com/investors. A replay of the call will be available for five days. Tapestry, Inc. is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol TPR.

data.title: US Foods (NYSE: USFD) to host Q3 2025 financial results conference call on Nov 6, 2025, at 8am CST. Dial 877-344-2001 to join. Conference ID: 2528845. Replay available.
data.description: US Foods Holding Corp. (NYSE: USFD) based in Rosemont, Illinois, is set to hold a conference call and webcast on November 6, 2025, at 8 a.m. CST to review their third quarter financial results. Those interested can join the call by dialing 877-344-2001, with a Conference ID of 2528845 required for connection. Participants are advised to dial in 10 minutes before the call's start time. A replay of the call will be accessible afterward. For more details on accessing the replay, interested parties should register.
